walkthrough.title: Unfold the Main Board
walkthrough.text: Let's start by unfolding the main board and placing it in the center of the table. The board shows all the different locations where you will be sending your workers throughout the game. Take a moment to look at the layout. You will see resource-gathering spots around the edges and key buildings like the Cathedral, the Guardhouse, and the Black Market.

walkthrough.title: Sort the Resource Tokens
walkthrough.text: Now let's organize all the resource tokens into separate piles near the board. You will find wood, stone, clay, gold, and silver tokens. Place each type in its own supply pile where everyone can reach them.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Building Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the large deck of building cards. Give them a good shuffle and place the deck face down near the Guild Hall area of the board. Then flip five building cards face up into a display row next to the deck. These are the buildings currently available for players to take.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Virtue Track
walkthrough.text: Let's find the virtue track along the edge of the board. Each player will place a marker on space seven, which is the starting position right in the middle. This track goes from zero to fourteen and will shift up or down based on your actions throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Cathedral
walkthrough.text: Locate the Cathedral contribution spaces on the board. Place the appropriate number of reward tiles on each contribution space based on your player count. The Cathedral is where players will deliver resources to earn victory points during the game.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle the Debt Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the small deck of debt cards and shuffle them. Place the deck face down near the board. These cards are worth negative two victory points each at the end of the game, so you will want to avoid accumulating too many of them.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Black Market
walkthrough.text: Let's prepare the Black Market. Place the Black Market board or tokens in its designated area. The Black Market offers cheaper resources but will cost you virtue every time you visit it. Draw the starting Black Market offerings face up.

walkthrough.title: Choose Player Colors
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a color and takes the matching set of twenty workers. These are the small figures in your chosen color that you will place one at a time on the board throughout the game. Keep all twenty workers in front of you for now.

walkthrough.title: Take Player Boards
walkthrough.text: Each player also takes a player board in their color. This board shows your personal building area where you will construct buildings throughout the game. It also has a handy reference for the different actions you can take on your turn.

walkthrough.title: Deal Starting Building Cards
walkthrough.text: Deal six building cards to each player from the building deck. Look at your cards but keep them hidden from the other players. These are the buildings you can construct during the game, though you will be able to pick up more from the Guild Hall as you play.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Starting Resources
walkthrough.text: Each player collects their starting resources from the supply. You should receive a small number of resources as shown on your player board reference. Make sure everyone has the same starting package so the game begins on equal footing.

walkthrough.title: Deal Apprentice Cards
walkthrough.text: Let's shuffle the apprentice cards and deal one to each player. Apprentices give you a special ability and a potential end-game scoring bonus. Keep your apprentice visible in front of you so everyone can see each other's special powers.

walkthrough.title: Select First Player
walkthrough.text: Pick a random player to go first. Play will proceed clockwise from the starting player. Unlike some games, there is no first-player marker that rotates. The game continues clockwise until an end-game condition is triggered.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how a turn works in Architects. On your turn, you do exactly one thing: place one worker from your personal supply onto any location on the board and take the action there. That is the entire turn. Then play passes clockwise to the next player.

walkthrough.title: Worker Stacking
walkthrough.text: Here is the key concept that makes this game unique. When you place a worker on a location, the action gets stronger based on how many of your own workers are already there. If it is your first worker at the Quarry, you gain one stone. Your second worker there gets you two stone. Your third gets three, and so on. Only your workers count toward this bonus, not other players' workers.

walkthrough.title: Gathering Resources: Quarry and Forest
walkthrough.text: Let's go over the resource locations. The Quarry gives you stone, and the Forest gives you wood. Both work the same way. Place a worker, and you receive resources equal to the number of your workers now at that location. These are the basic building materials you will need to construct buildings.

walkthrough.title: Gathering Resources: Mines and Silversmith
walkthrough.text: The Mines provide clay and gold, which are more valuable resources used for advanced buildings. The Silversmith gives you silver, which is the game's currency used for bailing out captured workers and paying off debts. Both of these locations follow the same stacking rules as the Quarry and Forest.

walkthrough.title: The Guild Hall
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about getting building cards. When you place a worker at the Guild Hall, you take one or more building cards from the face-up display into your hand. The number of cards you take depends on your worker count there, just like resources. After taking cards, refill the display from the deck.

walkthrough.title: The Workshop: Building
walkthrough.text: Now for the heart of the game. Place a worker at the Workshop to construct a building from your hand. Pay the resource cost shown on the building card, place it onto your player board, and score any immediate effects. Buildings are a major source of victory points and may give you ongoing bonuses.

walkthrough.title: The King's Storehouse: Cathedral Contributions
walkthrough.text: Place a worker at the King's Storehouse to contribute resources to the Cathedral. You pay the resource cost shown on an available Cathedral space, place your marker there, and earn victory points. Cathedral contributions are very valuable, and the limited spaces create a race among players to claim the best slots.

walkthrough.title: The Black Market
walkthrough.text: The Black Market lets you acquire resources at a discount, but there is a catch. Every time you visit the Black Market, your virtue drops by one on the virtue track. Let's be clear about the tradeoff: you get cheap resources now, but losing virtue can cost you significant points at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: The Virtue Track
walkthrough.text: Let's talk more about virtue. The track goes from zero to fourteen, and everyone starts at seven. Certain actions raise your virtue, like contributing to the Cathedral. Other actions lower it, like visiting the Black Market or taking debt cards. At the end of the game, high virtue earns bonus points and low virtue costs you points, while the middle of the track scores nothing.

walkthrough.title: Capturing Workers at the Town Centre
walkthrough.text: Here is the most aggressive action in the game. When you place a worker at the Town Centre, you capture all of one opponent's workers from a single location. Those captured workers go to the Tax Stand on the board. This is devastating because it strips the opponent of their stacking bonus at that location and forces them to spend time and silver recovering.

walkthrough.title: The Guardhouse: Jailing Workers
walkthrough.text: Place a worker at the Guardhouse to send workers to jail and earn silver for it. You can jail your own workers to thin out risky stacks, or in some cases jail captured opponents. This gives you silver income while removing workers from the board. Think of it as a way to profit from the capture-and-jail cycle.

walkthrough.title: The Tax Stand: Bailing Out Workers
walkthrough.text: Let's cover how you get your workers back. If your workers were captured and sent to the Tax Stand, you will need to spend a turn placing a worker there and paying silver to bail them out. You recover all of your captured workers at once, returning them to your personal supply so you can deploy them again.

walkthrough.title: Debt Cards
walkthrough.text: Some actions in the game give you debt cards, which are worth negative two victory points each at the end of the game. You can pay off debt cards during the game by spending silver or through certain actions. Let's make this clear: ignoring your debts can be very costly, so keep an eye on how many you have accumulated.

walkthrough.title: End-Game Triggers
walkthrough.text: The game ends when one of three conditions is met. First, if all the Cathedral contribution spaces are filled. Second, if a certain number of buildings have been constructed across all players. Third, if the debt card supply runs out. When any trigger occurs, you finish the current round so everyone gets equal turns.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: Let's add up those scores. You earn points from buildings you constructed, Cathedral contributions, your position on the virtue track, any gold you have left, and apprentice bonuses. Then subtract two points for every debt card you still hold. The player with the most points wins and earns the title of greatest architect of the West Kingdom.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Game
walkthrough.text: Let's walk through a practice game of Architects of the West Kingdom with four players. We will play out several rounds of turns, making some intentional mistakes along the way so you can learn what to watch out for. Follow along and you will feel confident heading into your first real game.

walkthrough.title: Round One: Gather Your First Resources
walkthrough.text: Let's start by sending a worker to the Quarry to pick up one stone. Since this is our first worker there, we only get one stone, but that is perfectly fine for now. Early turns are about spreading out and starting to collect what you need.

walkthrough.title: Build Up at the Forest
walkthrough.text: On our next turn, let's send a worker to the Forest for one wood. The other players are also spreading their workers around to different locations. Notice how everyone is being cautious about stacking too many workers in one spot early on.

walkthrough.title: Grab a Building Card
walkthrough.text: Let's visit the Guild Hall to grab a building card from the display. Look through the available buildings and pick one whose resource costs match what you are already collecting. Pay attention to the victory point values and any special bonuses the building might provide.

walkthrough.title: Stack Workers for Bigger Gains
walkthrough.text: Now let's send a second worker to the Quarry. Since we already have one worker there, this time we collect two stone. That stacking bonus is the engine of the game. The more you commit to a location, the bigger the payoff each visit.

walkthrough.title: Mistake One: Over-Stacking Workers
walkthrough.text: Let's keep going and send a third and fourth worker to the Quarry. Now we have four workers in one spot, which looks great for stone collection. But look at that pile of our workers sitting there. Any opponent can visit the Town Centre and capture all four of them in a single action.

walkthrough.title: The Capture Strikes
walkthrough.text: Sure enough, an opponent places a worker at the Town Centre and scoops up all four of our workers from the Quarry. They are now sitting at the Tax Stand, completely out of our control. We just lost four workers and our entire stacking investment at the Quarry in one devastating move.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Spread Your Workers
walkthrough.text: Let's learn from that mistake. Having three or four workers stacked on one location paints a huge target on your back. It is much safer to spread your workers across two or three locations rather than piling them all in one place. If you must stack high, make sure you have a plan to use the Guardhouse to jail your own workers before an opponent captures them.

walkthrough.title: Bail Out and Regroup
walkthrough.text: Now we need to spend a turn placing a worker at the Tax Stand and paying silver to bail out our four captured workers. This is a full turn wasted just getting our workers back, plus the silver cost. Let's pay up and get them back into our supply.

walkthrough.title: Get Some Silver
walkthrough.text: Let's visit the Silversmith to start building up our silver reserves. Silver is essential for bailing out workers, paying off debts, and fueling certain buildings. Having a steady silver supply keeps your options open throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Build Your First Building
walkthrough.text: We have enough resources now, so let's place a worker at the Workshop and construct a building. Pay the resource cost shown on the card, place the building on your player board, and collect any victory points or bonuses. This is the core of how you score in Architects.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Two: Ignoring Debt Cards
walkthrough.text: Let's visit the Black Market a few times to grab some cheap resources. Each visit gives us a debt card and lowers our virtue by one. After three visits, we are now holding three debt cards, which means negative six victory points at the end of the game if we do not pay them off.

walkthrough.title: Debt Piles Up Fast
walkthrough.text: We grabbed a fourth debt card from another action. That is now negative eight points sitting in front of us, which is enough to swing the entire game. Meanwhile, the other players each have zero or one debt card because they have been paying them off or avoiding the Black Market entirely.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Manage Your Debt
walkthrough.text: Let's learn from this. Debt cards are negative two victory points each, and they add up quickly. If you take a debt card, plan to pay it off soon rather than letting them accumulate. A couple of debts are manageable, but four or more can easily cost you the game. Budget silver specifically for debt repayment.

walkthrough.title: Mid-Game: Contribute to the Cathedral
walkthrough.text: Let's place a worker at the King's Storehouse and contribute to the Cathedral. Pay the required resources to claim a contribution space and score the victory points shown. Cathedral contributions are worth great points and also help push your virtue upward.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Three: Ignoring the Virtue Track
walkthrough.text: Let's check our virtue marker. After all those Black Market visits without any virtue-raising actions, we have dropped from seven down to three. Meanwhile, the other players are at eight, nine, and ten. At the end of the game, they will earn bonus points for high virtue while we are stuck in penalty range.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Commit to a Virtue Strategy
walkthrough.text: The virtue track rewards players who commit to the extremes. High virtue earns significant bonus points, and very low virtue also unlocks certain bonuses. But being stuck in the middle of the track scores you absolutely nothing. If you dip your virtue with Black Market visits, either commit to going low and embrace the dark side, or take actions to climb back up.

walkthrough.title: Late Game: Race for the Finish
walkthrough.text: As the game nears its end, the Cathedral spaces are filling up and buildings are being constructed rapidly. Let's focus our last few turns on building one more building and making a Cathedral contribution. Every remaining turn counts, so prioritize the highest-value actions you can take.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring Walkthrough
walkthrough.text: The game has ended. Let's tally up our score. We add points from buildings, Cathedral contributions, our virtue track position, leftover gold, and apprentice bonuses. Then we subtract two points per debt card. Even though we built well, our four unpaid debt cards and weak virtue position cost us dearly.

walkthrough.title: Play a Real Game
walkthrough.text: You now know the three biggest pitfalls in Architects: over-stacking workers in one spot, letting debt cards pile up, and drifting to the middle of the virtue track. Keep your workers spread out, pay off your debts, and commit to a virtue strategy. Now shuffle up those building cards and play a real game.

walkthrough.title: Diversify Your Worker Placement
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about the most important habit to build. Resist the urge to pile all your workers onto one location, even though the stacking bonus is tempting. Spread your workers across two or three resource locations so that if an opponent captures from one spot, you only lose two or three workers instead of five or six.

walkthrough.title: Keep Silver in Reserve
walkthrough.text: Silver is the most versatile resource in Architects. You need it to bail out captured workers, pay off debt cards, and fund certain buildings. Let's make a habit of visiting the Silversmith regularly so you always have a cushion. Running out of silver at the wrong moment can set you back multiple turns.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents' Worker Stacks
walkthrough.text: Let's keep an eye on where other players are piling up their workers. If an opponent has four or five workers on the Quarry, that is a prime capture target. Timing a Town Centre capture to steal a big stack can swing the game dramatically in your favor while crippling your opponent's resource engine.

walkthrough.title: Use the Guardhouse Proactively
walkthrough.text: Do not wait for opponents to capture your workers. If you have built up a large stack at a location and collected your resources, consider jailing your own workers at the Guardhouse before someone else captures them. You earn silver for jailing, and you deny your opponents the satisfaction of a big capture.

walkthrough.title: Commit to a Virtue Direction
walkthrough.text: The virtue track rewards extremes and punishes the middle. If you plan to use the Black Market heavily, lean into the low-virtue strategy and take advantage of the bonuses available at that end. If you want high virtue, focus on Cathedral contributions and avoid the Black Market entirely. Waffling in the middle means you score nothing from the track.

walkthrough.title: Plan Your Buildings Around Your Resources
walkthrough.text: When you pick up building cards from the Guild Hall, choose buildings whose resource costs align with what you are already collecting. If you have been gathering stone and wood, do not grab a building that requires gold and clay. Let's match our building plans to our resource strategy so we can construct efficiently without wasting turns.

walkthrough.title: Race for Cathedral Spaces
walkthrough.text: Cathedral contributions are some of the highest-value actions in the game, and the spaces are limited. Let's not wait too long to contribute. If you see the Cathedral filling up, prioritize getting your contributions in before the spaces run out. Missing out on the Cathedral entirely is a significant point loss.

walkthrough.title: Pay Off Debt Before It Snowballs
walkthrough.text: Every debt card is negative two victory points, and they accumulate faster than you think. Let's make it a rule to pay off debt cards within a turn or two of receiving them. If you let three or four debt cards sit unpaid, that is six to eight points you are throwing away, which is often the margin of victory.

bullets: Spread workers across 2–3 locations
Limits capture damage to 2–3 workers max

summary.title: Pay Off Debt Before It Snowballs

walkthrough.title: The Capture Economy
walkthrough.text: Let's elevate your game by weaponizing the capture mechanic. Instead of viewing captures as purely aggressive, think of them as an economic cycle. Capture an opponent's workers to disrupt their engine, then jail those workers at the Guardhouse for silver income. This funds your own debt payments and building plans while setting your opponent back multiple turns.

walkthrough.title: The Black Market Rush
walkthrough.text: An advanced strategy is to intentionally tank your virtue by hammering the Black Market early. Let's grab cheap resources rapidly to build expensive buildings ahead of schedule. The key is to pair this with the low-virtue bonuses available at the bottom of the track. This only works if you commit fully and do not try to climb back to the middle.

walkthrough.title: Cathedral Denial
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about a powerful defensive strategy. If you notice an opponent stockpiling resources for a Cathedral contribution, race to fill that space first. Cathedral denial forces your opponent to hold expensive resources they cannot use efficiently. Even if the contribution is not optimal for your own scoring, denying it to a leading player can be game-winning.

walkthrough.title: Apprentice Synergy
walkthrough.text: Your apprentice card defines a huge part of your strategy, so let's build around it from the very first turn. If your apprentice rewards you for building with stone, focus your workers on the Quarry and prioritize stone-heavy buildings. If your apprentice rewards Cathedral contributions, invest in those early. Ignoring your apprentice bonus is like leaving free points on the table.

walkthrough.title: Tempo and End-Game Timing
walkthrough.text: Let's control the pace of the game. If you are ahead, push toward end-game triggers by building rapidly or filling Cathedral spaces. If you are behind, slow the game down by diversifying your actions and avoiding triggers. Architects rewards players who can read the board state and accelerate or decelerate the game to their advantage.

walkthrough.title: The Self-Jail Cycle
walkthrough.text: Here is an advanced engine that experienced players use. Build up a stack of three or four workers at a resource location, harvest the big payout, then immediately jail your own workers at the Guardhouse for silver. This lets you benefit from high stacking while denying opponents the capture. Then retrieve your workers and repeat the cycle at a different location.

walkthrough.title: Resource Flexibility with Gold
walkthrough.text: Gold is the wild card resource in Architects. Let's keep some gold on hand because it can substitute for other resources in many building costs and is also worth victory points at the end of the game. Mining gold from the Mines gives you both a flexible resource and a scoring insurance policy if the game ends before you can spend it.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Debt Supply
walkthrough.text: Keep an eye on how many debt cards remain in the supply. If the debt supply is running low, the game could end suddenly when it empties. Let's use this knowledge to our advantage. If we are ahead, we can push opponents toward debt to accelerate the end trigger. If we are behind, we can avoid actions that deplete the debt supply to buy ourselves more time.

bullets: Capture opponent workers → jail at Guardhouse → earn silver
Disrupts opponent + funds your own plans

summary.title: The Black Market Rush
bullets: Tank virtue intentionally → cheap resources → fast builds
Only works if you commit to low-virtue bonuses

summary.title: Cathedral Denial
bullets: Fill Cathedral spaces before leading opponents can
Forces opponents to hold unusable resources

summary.title: Apprentice Synergy

Match resource gathering + buildings to apprentice rewards

summary.title: Tempo and End-Game Timing

Behind → slow down, diversify, avoid triggers

entries.definition: Worker-placement location that produces stone. Resources gained equal the number of your workers present.
Worker-placement location that produces wood. Resources gained equal the number of your workers present.
Worker-placement location that produces clay and gold. Resources gained equal the number of your workers present.
Worker-placement location that produces silver. Resources gained equal the number of your workers present.
Location where you construct buildings by paying their resource cost. Place a building card from your hand onto your player board.
Location where you draw building cards from the face-up display. Cards taken equal the number of your workers present.
Location where you contribute resources to the Cathedral. Pay the required resources to claim a contribution space and earn victory points.
Location where you capture all of one opponent's workers from a single location. Captured workers are sent to the Tax Stand.
Location where you jail workers and earn silver. Can jail your own workers proactively to avoid capture by opponents.
Location where captured workers are held. The owning player must spend a turn and pay silver to bail them out and return them to their supply.
Location where you acquire resources cheaply but lose one virtue per visit. May also give debt cards.
Basic resource gathered from the Forest. Used as a building material for many buildings.
Basic resource gathered from the Quarry. Used as a building material for many buildings.
Resource gathered from the Mines. Used in building construction, often for more advanced buildings.
Valuable resource gathered from the Mines. Can substitute for other resources in some building costs and is worth victory points at end of game.
Currency resource gathered from the Silversmith. Used to bail out captured workers, pay off debt cards, and fund certain buildings.
Scale from 0 to 14 tracking each player's moral standing. Starts at 7. High virtue earns end-game VP bonus; low virtue incurs VP penalty; middle scores nothing.
Certain actions raise your position on the virtue track, such as contributing to the Cathedral or specific building effects.
Actions that lower your virtue position, primarily visiting the Black Market. Losing too much virtue results in end-game VP penalties.
Players at the top of the virtue track earn significant bonus victory points at end of game. Rewards virtuous play throughout the game.
Players at the bottom of the virtue track lose victory points at end of game. Can be offset by low-virtue strategy bonuses in advanced play.
Each player has 20 workers in their color. One worker is placed per turn on a location to take an action.
Having multiple of your own workers at one location increases the action's strength. Your second worker at a spot gives double the base reward, third gives triple, etc.
Action at the Town Centre that takes all of one opponent's workers from a single location and sends them to the Tax Stand.
Action at the Tax Stand where you pay silver to recover all of your captured workers, returning them to your personal supply.
Sending workers to the Guardhouse. Earns silver and removes workers from the board. Can be used proactively on your own workers.
Cards showing structures you can construct at the Workshop by paying their resource cost. Primary source of victory points.
The five face-up building cards available to take from the Guild Hall. Refilled from the deck after cards are taken.
Personal board where constructed buildings are placed. Shows your building area and action reference.
Shared structure on the main board with limited contribution spaces. Contributing resources earns victory points and may raise virtue.
Cards worth -2 VP each at end of game. Acquired through Black Market visits and certain actions. Can be paid off with silver.
Special cards dealt one per player at setup. Provide a unique ability and end-game scoring bonus that shapes your overall strategy.
Three conditions that can end the game: Cathedral spaces full, building threshold reached, or debt card supply empty.
Sum of building VP + Cathedral contributions + virtue bonus/penalty + leftover gold + apprentice bonuses - (2 × debt cards held).
Core mechanic where you place one worker per turn on a board location to take that location's action. Architects uses a unique non-blocking variant.
The increasing reward gained by having multiple of your own workers at the same location. Key engine-building mechanic unique to Architects.
Strategy of developing increasingly powerful action combos over time. In Architects, stacking workers creates growing resource engines.
The pace at which the game progresses toward its end. Players can accelerate or decelerate by pushing or avoiding end-game triggers.
The concept of maximizing value from each turn. Losing a turn to bail out workers or pay off debt is poor action economy.
